questions.question: Prove that three points are collinear if AB + BC = AC.
questions.answer: Using distance formula, if the sum of two distances equals the third, the points lie on the same straight line.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other using coordinates.
questions.answer: By showing that midpoints of both diagonals are equal using midpoint formula.

questions.question: Verify that the given four points form a square using distance formula.
questions.answer: By showing all sides are equal and diagonals are equal.

questions.question: Prove that the midpoint of the hypotenuse of a right triangle is equidistant from all vertices.
questions.answer: Using distance formula, distances from midpoint to all vertices are equal.

questions.question: Find the ratio in which a point divides a line segment using section formula.
questions.answer: By equating coordinates using section formula and solving the ratio.

questions.question: Prove that the locus of points equidistant from two fixed points is a straight line.
questions.answer: The locus is the perpendicular bisector obtained using distance equality.

questions.question: Show that a triangle is right-angled using coordinates.
questions.answer: By verifying Pythagoras theorem using distance formula.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a triangle formed by collinear points is zero.
questions.answer: Since points lie on the same line, no enclosed area is formed.

questions.question: Explain how coordinates help in proving properties of a rhombus.
questions.answer: Using equal side lengths verified by distance formula.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a square are equal using coordinates.
questions.answer: By calculating diagonal lengths using distance formula.

questions.question: Derive section formula using coordinate axes.
questions.answer: By drawing perpendiculars and using similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain why midpoint of diagonals determines centre of parallelogram.
questions.answer: Because diagonals bisect each other at the centre.

questions.question: Prove that a triangle with equal sides is equilateral using coordinates.
questions.answer: By showing all three side lengths are equal using distance formula.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry simplifies geometric proofs.
questions.answer: It converts geometric relations into algebraic expressions.

questions.question: Prove that distance between two coincident points is zero.
questions.answer: Using distance formula, subtraction yields zero.

questions.question: Explain how to find coordinates of a point dividing a line externally.
questions.answer: External division is studied in higher classes using modified section formula.

questions.question: Prove that the midpoint of a line segment is equidistant from its endpoints.
questions.answer: Using distance formula, distances from midpoint to endpoints are equal.

questions.question: Explain how distance formula is used in navigation.
questions.answer: It helps calculate shortest paths between locations represented as coordinates.

questions.question: Prove that the distance between (a, b) and (−a, −b) is 2√(a² + b²).
questions.answer: By substituting coordinates in distance formula and simplifying.

questions.question: Explain how section formula helps in dividing land plots.
questions.answer: It helps locate dividing points in fixed ratios.

questions.question: Prove that the locus of a point equidistant from origin is a circle.
questions.answer: All points satisfying √(x² + y²) = r form a circle.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry is useful in engineering.
questions.answer: It aids in design, measurement and positioning.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal using coordinates.
questions.answer: By calculating diagonal lengths using distance formula.

questions.question: Explain why coordinate geometry is called analytical geometry.
questions.answer: Because it analyses geometry using algebra.

questions.question: Summarise the importance of coordinate geometry in mathematics.
questions.answer: It connects algebra and geometry, simplifying problem solving.
